prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `f0f8a26d`.
codex_response:
Rule summary: each foreground component is an odd-length horizontal or vertical segment on a uniform background; the output redraws every segment on a blank background with the same center, same length, and opposite orientation, so singletons stay fixed. I discarded the sonnet hint because it described rotating compound rectangular/L-shaped regions, which does not match the official examples; the opus hint matched and was used. The generator stays on-family by sampling sparse square `9x9`/`11x11`/`13x13` grids with one foreground color, disconnected odd-length segments, occasional singletons, and allowing transformed outputs to overlap or merge. No new shared primitive was needed.

Verification/build: official verifier passed `4/4`, the shared build produced `1000` examples, `./arc2_verify_generated_all.sh --task f0f8a26d` passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked `preview_01.png`.

Task package files: [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f0f8a26d/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f0f8a26d/verifier.py), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f0f8a26d/generator.py), [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f0f8a26d/notes.md)
